# Creditcarddebt Bankruptcy -- Chapter 7 & 13 Filing Guide [2026]
> Free Creditcarddebt bankruptcy guide for 2026. Compare Chapter 7 vs Chapter 13, local exemptions, filing costs, means test, and what t...

## About
creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- Balance Transfer Traps Before Bankruptcy: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/balance-transfers.html
- What to Do Before Filing Bankruptcy on Credit Card Debt: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/before-filing.html
- Chapter 13 and Credit Card Debt -- Repayment Plans: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/chapter-13.html
- Chapter 7 and Credit Card Debt -- How Discharge Works: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/chapter-7.html
- Rebuilding Credit After Bankruptcy Discharge: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/credit-after.html
- FAQ -- Credit Card Debt and Bankruptcy: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/faq.html
- Creditcarddebt Bankruptcy -- Chapter 7 & 13 Filing Guide: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/
- Credit Card Lawsuits and How Bankruptcy Stops Them: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/lawsuits.html
- Medical Debt Bankruptcy [2026] -- Can You Discharge Medical Bills?: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/medical-debt.html
- PPP Loan and Bankruptcy -- Can You Discharge PPP Debt?: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/ppp-loan-bankruptcy.html
- Back Rent in Bankruptcy -- Can You Discharge Rent Debt?: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/rent-owed-bankruptcy.html
- Secured vs Unsecured Credit Card Debt in Bankruptcy: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/secured-vs-unsecured.html
- Timeshare and Bankruptcy -- How to Get Out of a Timeshare: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/timeshare-bankruptcy.html
- Utility Bills in Bankruptcy -- Deposits, Shutoffs, and Discharge: https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/utility-bills-bankruptcy.html

## License
Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
